q: Two VMs in an availability set vs spread across availability zones — which SLA applies to zones?
e: Zone-spread multi-VM deployments get 99.99%; availability sets 99.95%; single premium-SSD VM 99.9%.

q: Interruptible batch rendering should minimize compute cost. Recommend:
e: Spot pricing suits fault-tolerant, interruptible workloads; never for production-critical services.

q: Which Functions plan eliminates cold starts with pre-warmed instances and adds VNet integration?
e: Premium keeps warm instances, supports VNet and unlimited duration — for latency-sensitive event apps.

q: Fan-out/fan-in of 500 parallel activity functions with aggregated results requires…
e: Durable Functions patterns: chaining, fan-out/fan-in, async HTTP, monitors, human interaction.

q: An AKS API server must not be reachable from the internet. Configure:
e: Private AKS clusters place the control-plane endpoint on a private IP (Private Link).

q: Pods on AKS need Entra tokens to access Key Vault without stored secrets. Use:
o: Node managed identity for all pods
e: Workload identity federates K8s service accounts to Entra — per-pod identity, no secrets.

q: Massively parallel Monte-Carlo simulation across thousands of cores, job-based. Best service:
e: Batch schedules large-scale parallel/HPC jobs on managed pools (Spot-capable).

q: Which VM cost lever provides discounts while retaining flexibility to change VM families and regions?
e: Savings plans commit to $/hr spend flexibly; reservations lock family/region for deeper discounts.

q: Which of the 5 Rs describes moving VMs as-is to Azure IaaS?
e: Rehost = lift-and-shift; fastest migration, least cloud optimization.

q: Before migrating 300 on-prem VMs you need discovery, dependency mapping, right-sizing, and cost estimates. Use:
e: Azure Migrate handles discovery/assessment including dependency visualization and sizing.

q: Minimal-downtime online migration of SQL Server databases to SQL MI uses…
e: DMS online mode continuously syncs until cutover, minimizing downtime.

q: You must move 800 TB to Azure from a site with poor bandwidth. Recommend:
e: Data Box ships encrypted appliances — offline bulk transfer when network is impractical.

q: Orchestrating nightly ETL copying data from on-prem SQL + SaaS APIs into a lake calls for…
o: Azure Data Factory with self-hosted integration runtime
e: ADF orchestrates hybrid ETL; the self-hosted IR reaches on-prem sources.

q: Real-time SQL queries over Event Hubs streams with windowing (tumbling/hopping) suggests…
e: Stream Analytics is SQL-on-streams with native windowing functions.

q: Sub-second interactive analytics over billions of telemetry rows (KQL) is the sweet spot of…
e: ADX/Data Explorer powers telemetry/time-series analytics — the same engine behind Log Analytics.

q: Business analysts need a unified SaaS platform: lakehouse, warehouse, real-time, and Power BI in one. Recommend:
e: Fabric unifies analytics workloads over OneLake — Microsoft's strategic analytics direction.

q: Low-code integration across Salesforce, SAP, and on-prem systems with connectors and approval steps:
e: Logic Apps' 1400+ connectors and designer suit workflow/B2B integration; code-first orchestration → Durable Functions.

q: Key Vault must guarantee deleted secrets/vaults can't be permanently removed during retention (CMK requirement). Enable:
e: Purge protection blocks permanent deletion until retention expires — mandatory for CMK encryption scenarios.

q: FIPS 140-2 Level 3 dedicated HSM control of keys requires…
e: Managed HSM is the dedicated, single-tenant FIPS 140-2 L3 offering; Premium uses shared HSMs.

q: Which Key Vault authorization model does Microsoft recommend for new vaults?
e: RBAC unifies management, supports PIM/conditions, and replaces legacy per-vault access policies.

q: Governance must extend Azure Policy and RBAC to VMs running in AWS and on-premises. Use:
e: Arc projects external machines into ARM so Policy, RBAC, tags, Monitor, and Defender apply.

q: Which IaC feature blocks out-of-band portal edits to deployed resources and cleans up removed ones?
e: Deployment stacks manage deployments as units with deny settings — the Blueprints successor.

q: Bicep vs Terraform — which statement is accurate?
o: Bicep is Azure-native (day-0 ARM support, no state file); Terraform is multicloud with state management
e: Bicep compiles to ARM with no separate state; Terraform brings multicloud + ecosystem with state files.

q: CI/CD should deploy to Azure with zero stored cloud credentials. Configure:
o: Service principal secret in repo settings
OIDC workload identity federation from GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps
e: Federated OIDC exchanges pipeline tokens for Entra tokens — no long-lived secrets to leak or rotate.

q: Which managed service load-tests your API with JMeter/Locust scripts in CI/CD?
e: Azure Load Testing runs managed large-scale load tests and integrates with pipelines for regression gates.

q: Requirement: 'store app feature flags and non-secret settings centrally with Key Vault references for secrets.' Recommend:
o: Azure App Configuration + Key Vault references
e: App Configuration handles settings/feature flags; secrets stay in Key Vault via references.

q: A workload needs 99.99% availability. Single-region design maxes at 99.95% due to a component. Next step per WAF?
o: Add zone/regional redundancy for that component or adjust the requirement with stakeholders
e: Architects surface the gap: redesign the weakest serial component or renegotiate the target.

q: Case study: 'minimize administrative effort' for SQL HA across regions with app transparency. Best answer:
e: 'Minimize admin effort' + transparency = failover groups with listener endpoints — no app changes.

q: Which combination satisfies 'analyze last month's platform logs cheaply, rarely queried, kept 5 years'?
o: Log Analytics with archive/long-term retention tier (restore/search jobs when needed)
e: Long-term/archive tiers store cheaply; search/restore jobs rehydrate on demand.

q: An architect must pick between App Service, Container Apps, and AKS for 12 microservices; team has no K8s skills; needs Dapr, scale-to-zero. Recommend:
e: ACA delivers microservice capabilities (Dapr, KEDA, revisions) without K8s operational burden.

q: Which service estimates savings from moving on-prem servers to Azure including power/real-estate costs?
e: TCO Calculator compares total on-prem cost vs Azure; Pricing Calculator prices planned Azure architectures.
